audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for sheepfold, door, good shepherd, one flock, Dedication feast, Father/Son oneness, hand imagery, Psalm gods quotation, Jordan return.

verses.restored: Amen, amen, I say to you: the one not entering through the door into the sheepfold, but climbing up another way, that one is a thief and robber.
But the one entering through the door is shepherd of the sheep.
To this one the gatekeeper opens, and the sheep hear his voice; he calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
When he has brought out all his own, he goes before them, and the sheep follow him because they know his voice.
A stranger they will certainly not follow, but they will flee from him, because they do not know the voice of strangers.
Jesus spoke this figure of speech to them, but they did not know what the things were that he was speaking to them.
Jesus therefore said again, Amen, amen, I say to you: I am the door of the sheep.
All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not hear them.
I am the door. If anyone enters through me, that one will be saved/healed and will go in and go out and find pasture.
The thief does not come except to steal, kill, and destroy. I came so that they may have life and have abundance.
I am the beautiful/good shepherd. The beautiful/good shepherd lays down his life/soul for the sheep.
The hired worker, not being shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, sees the wolf coming, leaves the sheep, and flees; the wolf seizes them and scatters them.
He flees because he is a hired worker and it does not matter to him about the sheep.
I am the beautiful/good shepherd, and I know my own, and my own know me,
just as the Father knows me and I know the Father; and I lay down my life/soul for the sheep.
I have other sheep that are not from this fold; it is necessary for me to lead those also, and they will hear my voice, and there will be one flock, one shepherd.
Because of this the Father loves me, because I lay down my life/soul so that I may take it again.
No one takes it from me; rather I lay it down from myself. I have authority to lay it down, and I have authority to take it again. This command I received from my Father.
Again division happened among the Judeans because of these words.
Many of them were saying, He has a troubling spirit and is mad; why hear him?
Others said, These are not the words of one troubled by a spirit. Is a troubling spirit able to open the eyes of the blind?
Then the Dedication happened in Jerusalem; it was winter.
Jesus was walking in the temple, in the portico of Solomon.
The Judeans therefore surrounded him and were saying to him, How long do you keep our soul/life suspended? If you are the Messiah, tell us openly.
Jesus answered them, I told you, and you do not trust. The works that I do in my Father's name, these bear witness concerning me.
But you do not trust, because you are not from my sheep.
My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.
I give them age-long life, and they will certainly not perish into the age, and no one will seize them out of my hand.
My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to seize out of the Father's hand.
I and the Father are one.
The Judeans again picked up stones so that they might stone him.
Jesus answered them, Many beautiful/good works I showed you from the Father; because of which work among them do you stone me?
The Judeans answered him, We do not stone you concerning a beautiful/good work, but concerning blasphemy, and because you, being human, make yourself Divine.
Jesus answered them, Has it not been written in your Law, I said, you are gods/divine ones?
If he called those gods/divine ones to whom the word of the Divine came, and the Scripture cannot be broken,
do you say of the one whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, You blaspheme, because I said, I am Son of the Divine?
If I do not do the works of my Father, do not trust me.
But if I do them, even if you do not trust me, trust the works, so that you may know and keep knowing that the Father is in me and I in the Father.
Therefore they were seeking again to seize him, and he went out from their hand.
He went away again beyond the Jordan to the place where John was baptizing at first, and he remained there.
Many came to him and were saying, John indeed did no sign, but everything John said about this one was true.
And many there trusted into him.
